goal: Add a contract-only Bluepilot surface for the GOAT Desktop local /builder-cue proposal boundary without calling GOAT, capturing screens, emitting popup proposals, or executing desktop actions.
scope_out: Do not call GOAT Desktop, capture screenshots, create popup proposals, move mouse, type keys, execute runtime, call providers, write durable state, merge, deploy, or create a GitHub PR from application code.
claims: Bluepilot can describe the GOAT Desktop local /builder-cue proposal contract as read-only JSON.
Bluepilot can dry-validate builder-cue payload shape before any later local bridge call.
Vision-only geometry remains rejected and all desktop effects remain false.
assumptions: Actual GOAT bridge calls require a later explicit activation lock and local operator review.
GOAT Desktop remains the authority for active-window verification and popup approval.
dod: POST /probe/goat-desktop-builder-cue-preflight validates local builder-cue payloads without calling GOAT.
The eight-point readiness and operator dashboard mark GOAT as wired_contract_only.
Focused tests, typecheck, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: A live GOAT call, screen capture, desktop action, provider call, runtime execution, DB write, durable write, merge, deploy, package change, or workflow change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: The eight-point integration surface represented GOAT Desktop as locked_ready_for_review; local GOAT docs and code show /builder-cue is proposal-only and requires source, action_type, label, and bbox.
